mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates broad economic strength, which translates into higher expected corporate earnings and drives equity prices up over the medium term.
evidence_refs: fact:Post-GFC recovery shows steady job gains as of mid-2013.
failure_condition: The rank information coefficient between the 3-month change in PAYEMS and 20-day forward returns is consistently below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year CPI inflation accelerates past a critical threshold, markets anticipate a hawkish Federal Reserve response, leading to higher expected future interest rates, compressing equity multiples and causing negative forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:The 'Taper Tantrum' of May-June 2013 demonstrates market sensitivity to perceived shifts in Fed policy.
failure_condition: S&P 500 returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where YoY CPI exceeds 2.5% over a 60-day forward horizon.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of accelerating retail sales growth (RSAFS) and a declining unemployment rate (UNRATE) indicates robust consumer health. This dual strength in spending and employment boosts revenue expectations for consumer-facing companies, driving the S&P 500 higher.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending is the primary driver of US GDP.
failure_condition: A model combining both series fails to outperform a model using only RSAFS or UNRATE individually based on out-of-sample Sharpe ratio over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recessionary environment, a positive surprise or strong acceleration in industrial production (INDPRO) indicates that the supply side of the economy is recovering faster than expected, leading to upward revisions in earnings forecasts and positive equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:The economy in 2013 is in a prolonged recovery phase from the 2008 Global Financial Crisis.
failure_condition: The signal shows no predictive power (Rank IC close to zero) during periods more than 4 years after a NBER-defined recession trough.

mechanism_narrative: When the 3-month moving average of the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises by 0.5 percentage points or more from its 12-month low, it signals a high probability of recession, triggering investor flight to safety and causing sharp declines in the S&P 500.
failure_condition: In more than 30% of triggered instances, the S&P 500 posts positive 60-day forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in housing starts (HOUST) signals falling demand for big-ticket items and construction activity, which has a strong negative multiplier effect on the broader economy. This leads to lower GDP growth expectations and negative equity market performance.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing sector weakness was a key leading indicator for the 2008 GFC.
failure_condition: The correlation between the 6-month change in HOUST and 60-day forward S&P 500 returns is positive over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the Fed Funds rate is pinned near the zero lower bound, any significant uptick in CPI inflation creates policy uncertainty. Markets price in a faster-than-expected exit from accommodation, leading to higher bond yields and lower equity valuations.
evidence_refs: fact:As of June 2013, the US is in a prolonged Zero Interest Rate Policy (ZIRP) period.
failure_condition: The signal has no predictive power (Rank IC near zero) during periods when FEDFUNDS is above 1%.

mechanism_narrative: The release of quarterly real GDP data (GDPC1) showing growth acceleration relative to the previous quarter provides a strong confirmation of economic momentum. This reduces uncertainty and causes portfolio managers to increase equity allocations, driving prices up over a short horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:GDP is the broadest measure of economic activity and its release is a major market event.
failure_condition: The average 5-day S&P 500 return following GDP releases that show quarter-over-quarter acceleration is not statistically different from the average return on other days (t-test p > 0.1).
